channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: ⚔️🇮🇷 Iran strikes US bases in Jordan and Gulf after Trump orders attacks near Hormuz
item.description: Iran launched strikes against US military bases in Jordan and the Gulf region in retaliation for American attacks near the strategic Strait of Hormuz, further straining an already fragile ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇹🇼🚀 Taiwan test fires US rocket system toward Chinese mainland for first time
item.description: Taiwan fired HIMARS rockets toward China in its first such test, a significant escalation of military demonstrations as cross-strait tensions continue to rise.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine launches long-range strikes on Russian military and energy sites
item.description: Ukrainian forces conducted coordinated long-range strikes against military facilities and energy infrastructure inside Russia, demonstrating continued capability to project power deep behind enemy lines.

item.title: 🇿🇦💔 Manhunt underway in South Africa after 12 killed in Johannesburg mass shooting
item.description: South African police are hunting multiple gunmen who opened fire in a late-night attack in Johannesburg, killing 12 people and wounding nine others in one of the country deadliest mass shootings.

item.title: 🇬🇷🏛️ Greece approves faster migrant returns and plans return hubs
item.description: The Greek parliament approved legislation to accelerate the return of migrants who do not qualify for asylum and aims to establish return processing hubs, part of broader EU efforts to manage migration flows.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇯🇵 Philippines to pursue maritime border talks with Japan despite China opposition
item.description: Manila plans to delimit sea boundaries with Tokyo, a move Beijing opposes as both nations face separate maritime disputes with China in the East China Sea and South China Sea.

item.title: ⚔️ Pentagon reveals preferred munitions for one-way attack drones
item.description: The US Defense Department has published a list of preferred payloads for loitering munitions, with Northrop Grumman and several startups competing to supply low-cost warheads compatible with various drone platforms.

item.title: 🇰🇼🛡️ US approves $2 billion counter-drone sale to Kuwait after Iranian strikes
item.description: The State Department cleared a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms to Kuwait, following recent Iranian attacks on the Gulf state amid escalating regional conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea warned on fragmented air defenses amid drone swarm threat
item.description: Military and industry experts urged Seoul to urgently build an integrated air and missile defense system to counter evolving drone swarm threats from North Korea.

item.title: 💰🌏 Dollar rally compounds economic pain for Asian nations amid Iran war oil shock
item.description: Asian countries already struggling with high oil prices from the Middle East conflict face additional pressure from a strengthening US dollar, increasing import costs and fiscal strains.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Crimea faces fuel shortages as Ukrainian drones disrupt Russian supply routes
item.description: Residents in Russian-controlled Crimea are grappling with gasoline rationing after Ukrainian drone attacks constricted supplies from Russia, with one witness describing severe disruptions to logistics in the annexed peninsula.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan to raise rates to 1.0% as inflation pressures mount
item.description: The BOJ is set to raise its key interest rate this month and again in Q4, reaching 1.25% by year-end, as it becomes more wary of inflation risks from energy shocks and a weak yen.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China prepares $295 billion AI infrastructure buildout to challenge US dominance
item.description: Beijing is planning to spend around 2 trillion yuan over five years on data center infrastructure across the country, accelerating its competition with Washington in the AI race.

item.title: 🇦🇫 Taliban Crack Down on Anti-Hijab Protest in Western Afghanistan
item.description: Afghan security forces dispersed a women's rights protest in Herat after Taliban morality police detained women for violating dress codes, marking another escalation in the regime's restrictions on women's freedoms.

item.title: 💰 Dollar Holds Steady as Markets Brace for US-Iran Tensions and Inflation Data
item.description: The US dollar remained stable against major currencies as investors monitored escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran while awaiting key inflation data that could influence Federal Reserve rate decisions.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Launches Deadly Strikes on Lebanon as Trump Seeks Peace Deal
item.description: Israeli attacks killed at least 17 people in southern Lebanon after evacuation orders in Tyre, even as former President Trump claimed the US-Iran peace deal was in its "final throes" amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Urges Restraint After US Strikes on Iran Raise Middle East Tensions
item.description: Beijing expressed deep concern over escalating Middle East conflict following US military strikes on Iran, calling for de-escalation as regional tensions surge after Washington's retaliation for the downing of an American helicopter.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore PM Lawrence Wong to Visit Russia, First Trip Since Ukraine War Sanctions
item.description: Singapore-Russia relations deteriorated after the city-state imposed sanctions on Moscow over its invasion of Ukraine. This marks a significant diplomatic visit amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: 🇭🇰 Hong Kong Debt Market Surges as Tencent and Swire Raise $5.2 Billion in Multi-Currency Deals
item.description: Investor appetite for Hong Kong debt is growing, with multi-currency offerings reinforcing the financial hub role.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Launches Riyadh Air Despite Regional War and Delays
item.description: Saudi Arabia launched its second national carrier after more than a year of delays, defying regional tensions and demonstrating continued investment in aviation infrastructure.

item.title: ⚛️ Portable Nuclear Reactors Move Closer to Powering US Military Bases During Outages
item.description: The first fueled criticality test of a microreactor in a military program validates technology for reducing reliance on vulnerable power grids at forward operating bases.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa Mass Shooting: 12 Killed in Johannesburg Informal Settlement
item.description: Multiple attackers opened fire in the Jumpers informal settlement in Cleveland, Johannesburg, killing 12 and wounding 9. Police have launched a manhunt for over 10 suspects.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Signs Drone Deal With Latvia as Zelenskiy Meets Nordic-Baltic Leaders
item.description: Ukraine has signed a drone cooperation agreement with Latvia during a summit with Nordic and Baltic states in Tallinn, Estonia. President Zelenskiy met with Latvian PM Kulbergs to discuss military cooperation.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇲🇾 Malaysia to Supply Maximum LNG and Naphtha to Japan Under New Agreement
item.description: Malaysia will provide Japan with maximum possible LNG and naphtha supplies as Tokyo seeks to diversify energy sources amid regional instability. The agreement comes as Japan faces energy security concerns.

item.title: 🇸🇳⚽ Senegal Football Federation Explains Viral Airport Security Footage Before World Cup
item.description: Senegal football federation issued a statement explaining security footage of their World Cup squad undergoing checks on an airport tarmac, after the images prompted accusations of discriminatory treatment.
